endpoint.label: Claude Opus 4.7
endpoint.summary: Send synchronous chat completion requests to Claude Opus 4.7 through APIDot's OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint.
endpoint.seoDescription: Reference the Claude Opus 4.7 OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint on APIDot, including request shape, response fields, pricing notes, and examples.
endpoint.about: Use `/v1/chat/completions` when you want OpenAI-compatible chat integration with `claude-opus-4-7`. APIDot also supports the same model through `/v1/messages` for Anthropic-native request payloads, but this docs entry focuses on the chat completions path shown in the page API tab.
bestPractices: Keep system instructions explicit because Claude Opus 4.7 is more literal than earlier models and performs best with clear success criteria.
Monitor prompt and completion token usage closely because pricing is split between input and output tokens, and longer reasoning chains can materially increase spend.
Use `/v1/messages` instead when your stack already depends on Anthropic-native request semantics or future Anthropic-specific features.
For screenshot, chart, and document workflows, pass multimodal content through the endpoint variant your backend already standardizes on, while keeping the model id `claude-opus-4-7` unchanged.

faq.question: Which model id should I send?
How much does Claude Opus 4.7 cost on this page?
faq.answer: Pricing is aligned to the current PoYo reference page: $4 per 1M input tokens and $20 per 1M output tokens.

faq.question: Does APIDot also support `/v1/messages` for Claude Opus 4.7?
faq.answer: Yes. `/v1/messages` is supported for the same model id, but this endpoint entry documents the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` path.

faq.question: What context and output limits should I expect?
faq.answer: Claude Opus 4.7 is publicly positioned around a 1M token context window and up to 128K output tokens, depending on endpoint behavior and account limits.

request.name: messages
request.description: Conversation array in OpenAI-compatible shape. Include `system`, `user`, and `assistant` roles as needed.

request.name: messages[].role
request.description: Message role. Common values are `system`, `user`, and `assistant`.

request.name: messages[].content
request.description: Plain text message content for the corresponding role.

request.name: temperature
request.description: Sampling temperature. Lower values bias toward more deterministic output.

request.name: max_tokens
request.description: Maximum completion tokens to return in the response.

request.name: top_p
request.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. Keep `1` unless your stack already standardizes a lower value.

response.name: model
response.description: Resolved model id used to serve the request.

response.name: usage.prompt_tokens
response.description: Input token count billed at the input rate.

response.name: usage.completion_tokens
response.description: Output token count billed at the output rate.

response.name: usage.total_tokens
response.description: Total token count across input and output.

errors.name: invalid_request
errors.description: Missing model, empty messages array, invalid role values, or malformed numeric parameters.

errors.name: authentication_error
errors.description: Missing, expired, or invalid Bearer API key.

errors.name: insufficient_credits
errors.description: Your prepaid balance cannot cover the request's token usage.

errors.name: rate_limited
errors.description: The API key is temporarily above the allowed request rate.

messages.content: You are a professional AI assistant.
Analyze this codebase and suggest improvements.
